title: Compute Names and Descriptions Deliberately
summary: Build and verify accessible names descriptions in ambiguous survey control naming audit through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain accessible names descriptions from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply accessible names descriptions without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Ambiguous survey control naming audit case 1: adding aria-label improves every element even when visible text already names it.
Ambiguous survey control naming audit case 1: prefer visible native labels, predict the name-computation result, and add a separate description only when supporting detail is needed.
Ambiguous survey control naming audit case 1: repeat the label text in aria-label and aria-describedby to guarantee three announcements.

steps.title: 02 · Read the ambiguous survey control naming audit case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 2: Which claim preserves accessible names descriptions when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: ambiguous survey control naming audit.
steps.why: Compute Names and Descriptions Deliberately makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 2 in ambiguous survey control naming audit.

content.text: An accessible name identifies an element’s purpose; a description supplies supporting detail. Visible label text usually provides the clearest control name. Aria-labelledby references visible naming text, aria-label supplies a name when no suitable visible label exists, and aria-describedby connects help or error details. Conflicting naming sources can hide useful visible wording. Apply it to ambiguous survey control naming audit.
